content.text:
You are applying code migration fixes to a single file.

File: /home/jmontleo/semver-analyzer/build/batch-run-20260604T185102/nmstate-console-plugin/source/cypress/support/selectors.ts

Apply ALL of the following 4 fixes to this file:

### Fix 1
Line: 7
Rule [sd-css-dead-class-pf-v5-c-dropdown]:
The CSS class 'pf-v5-c-dropdown' was removed in the new version. A simple version prefix swap to 'pf-v6-c-dropdown' does NOT produce a valid class — this class does not exist in the target CSS distribution.

Remove this class reference or replace it with appropriate custom CSS or a PatternFly component prop.

Incident context:
  matchingText: button.pf-v5-c-dropdown__toggle[data-test-id="dropdown-button"]

Code context:
```
    6  export const SEARCH_FILTER_DROPDOWN =
    7    'button.pf-v5-c-dropdown__toggle[data-test-id="dropdown-button"]';

```

### Fix 2
Line: 7
Rule [sd-css-dead-class-pf-v5-c-dropdown-toggle]:
The CSS class 'pf-v5-c-dropdown__toggle' was removed in the new version. A simple version prefix swap to 'pf-v6-c-dropdown__toggle' does NOT produce a valid class — this class does not exist in the target CSS distribution.

Remove this class reference or replace it with appropriate custom CSS or a PatternFly component prop.

Incident context:
  matchingText: button.pf-v5-c-dropdown__toggle[data-test-id="dropdown-button"]

Code context:
```
    6  export const SEARCH_FILTER_DROPDOWN =
    7    'button.pf-v5-c-dropdown__toggle[data-test-id="dropdown-button"]';

```

### Fix 3
Line: 7
Rule [semver-css-class-removed-c-dropdown]:
The CSS class 'pf-v5-c-dropdown' has no equivalent in the new version. There is no valid 'pf-v6-c-dropdown' class — this class was removed, not renamed.

Remove this class reference or replace it with appropriate custom CSS or a component prop.

Incident context:
  matchingText: button.pf-v5-c-dropdown__toggle[data-test-id="dropdown-button"]

Code context:
```
    6  export const SEARCH_FILTER_DROPDOWN =
    7    'button.pf-v5-c-dropdown__toggle[data-test-id="dropdown-button"]';

```

### Fix 4
Line: 7
Rule [semver-css-class-removed-c-dropdown-toggle]:
The CSS class 'pf-v5-c-dropdown__toggle' has no equivalent in the new version. There is no valid 'pf-v6-c-dropdown__toggle' class — this class was removed, not renamed.

Remove this class reference or replace it with appropriate custom CSS or a component prop.

Incident context:
  matchingText: button.pf-v5-c-dropdown__toggle[data-test-id="dropdown-button"]

Code context:
```
    6  export const SEARCH_FILTER_DROPDOWN =
    7    'button.pf-v5-c-dropdown__toggle[data-test-id="dropdown-button"]';

```

Instructions:
1. Read the file at /home/jmontleo/semver-analyzer/build/batch-run-20260604T185102/nmstate-console-plugin/source/cypress/support/selectors.ts
2. Process each fix INDEPENDENTLY in sequence. For each fix:
   a. Identify the exact code affected (line number and affected element)
   b. Determine the specific change needed (add/remove/rename identifiers, update references)
   c. Track all changes for the final write
3. Make the minimum edits necessary — do not change unrelated code, but DO clean up any artifacts caused by your changes (e.g., remove imports that are no longer referenced, delete dead declarations)
4. After determining your changes, reason through any functional side effects: verify that your edits preserve the existing behavior of the surrounding code. If a migration change makes existing variables, state, or logic redundant, clean up the affected code so the file remains correct and consistent.
5. Do NOT revert any changes that were already applied in previous passes
6. Write the fixed file once with ALL changes from every fix applied
7. REQUIRED: If companion test files are listed above, read EACH one now. Do NOT skip this step. For each test file, check whether your changes to the main file would cause test failures (e.g., portal rendering means getByText/getByRole won't find dropdown content, renamed attributes break selectors). If a test would break, apply the fix and write the updated test file.


Before writing, reason through each fix step by step to ensure nothing is missed. Then read the file, make the edits, and write it.

After writing the file, produce a '## Changes Applied' section that lists each change you made, each fix that was already applied (no change needed), and each fix you could not apply (with a brief reason). This summary is used by subsequent processing steps.

I'll start by analyzing all 4 fixes, then read the file to apply them.

**Analysis of fixes:**
- All 4 fixes target the same line (line 7) and the same string: `'button.pf-v5-c-dropdown__toggle[data-test-id="dropdown-button"]'`
- Fix 1 & 3: The CSS class `pf-v5-c-dropdown` was removed (no v6 equivalent)
- Fix 2 & 4: The CSS class `pf-v5-c-dropdown__toggle` was removed (no v6 equivalent)
- In PatternFly v6, the old `Dropdown` component was moved to `deprecated/` and replaced with a new `Dropdown` based on `MenuToggle`. The new dropdown uses `pf-v6-c-menu-toggle` instead of the old `pf-v5-c-dropdown__toggle`.

The selector `button.pf-v5-c-dropdown__toggle[data-test-id="dropdown-button"]` is a Cypress test selector targeting a dropdown toggle button. The v6 replacement class is `pf-v6-c-menu-toggle`.

Let me read the file:

Now let me reason through the fixes:

**All 4 fixes target the same line 7** — the CSS selector string `'button.pf-v5-c-dropdown__toggle[data-test-id="dropdown-button"]'`.

- `pf-v5-c-dropdown` and `pf-v5-c-dropdown__toggle` are both removed in PF v6 with no direct equivalent.
- In PatternFly v6, the Dropdown component was rebuilt on top of `MenuToggle`. The toggle button now uses the class `pf-v6-c-menu-toggle` instead of `pf-v5-c-dropdown__toggle`.
- This is a Cypress test selector, so it needs to match the actual DOM element. The PF v6 `MenuToggle` renders as a `button.pf-v6-c-menu-toggle`.
- The `[data-test-id="dropdown-button"]` attribute selector should be preserved since it's a custom test ID.

The correct replacement selector is: `'button.pf-v6-c-menu-toggle[data-test-id="dropdown-button"]'`

The file looks correct.

## Changes Applied

| Fix | Rule | Status | Detail |
|-----|------|--------|--------|
| Fix 1 | `sd-css-dead-class-pf-v5-c-dropdown` | ✅ Applied | Replaced `pf-v5-c-dropdown__toggle` CSS class selector with `pf-v6-c-menu-toggle` — the PF v6 Dropdown is built on `MenuToggle`, so the toggle button now uses the `pf-v6-c-menu-toggle` class. This resolves the removal of the `pf-v5-c-dropdown` block class. |
| Fix 2 | `sd-css-dead-class-pf-v5-c-dropdown-toggle` | ✅ Applied | Same edit — the `__toggle` BEM element class `pf-v5-c-dropdown__toggle` was replaced by the standalone `pf-v6-c-menu-toggle` class. |
| Fix 3 | `semver-css-class-removed-c-dropdown` | ✅ Applied | Same edit addresses this rule — the `pf-v5-c-dropdown` block reference is fully removed from the selector. |
| Fix 4 | `semver-css-class-removed-c-dropdown-toggle` | ✅ Applied | Same edit addresses this rule — the `pf-v5-c-dropdown__toggle` element reference is fully removed from the selector. |

**Summary:** All 4 fixes targeted the same selector string on line 7. A single edit replaced `button.pf-v5-c-dropdown__toggle` with `button.pf-v6-c-menu-toggle`, which is the correct PF v6 equivalent (the v6 Dropdown component uses `MenuToggle` internally, rendering a `<button class="pf-v6-c-menu-toggle">`). The `[data-test-id="dropdown-button"]` attribute selector was preserved unchanged.
